sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see! UP moved the W-box UP by 3 rows. It was at rows 30-32 and is now at rows 27-29. So the W-box moves in steps of 3 rows (its own height). Similarly, DOWN would move it down by 3 rows.
So the W-box can move in all 4 directions:
- LEFT/RIGHT: moves horizontally by 1 column
- UP/DOWN: moves vertically by 3 rows (its height)
The left charcoal box is at rows 32-36, cols 11-15. Its interior is at rows 33-35, cols 12-14.
The W-box is now at rows 27-29, cols 9-11.
To get the W-box into the charcoal box interior (rows 33-35, cols 12-14), I need to:
1. Move DOWN to get to rows 30-32 (one DOWN)
2. Then I need to get to rows 33-35... but DOWN moves by 3 rows, so from rows 30-32, DOWN would go to rows 33-35. That's exactly where the charcoal box interior is!
3. Move RIGHT to align cols 12-14
Wait, let me re-check. The W-box is currently at rows 27-29. If I press DOWN, it moves to rows 30-32. If I press DOWN again, it moves to rows 33-35. That would put it at the same rows as the charcoal box interior.
But the charcoal box is at cols 11-15, and the W-box is at cols 9-11. I need to move RIGHT by 2 to get to cols 11-13, which would overlap with the charcoal box.
- W-box center: (28, 10)
- Charcoal box interior: rows 33-35, cols 12-14
- To align: DOWN twice (27-29 → 33-35) and RIGHT twice (9-11 → 13-15)
But I need to verify the DOWN behavior first—earlier it only changed the HUD bar, so I should check if that was because the W-box was in a different position or if something else is affecting the movement.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# W-box is at rows 27-29, cols 9-11
# Charcoal box interior is at rows 33-35, cols 12-14
# Need: DOWN twice (27→30→33) and RIGHT twice (9→13)
# But earlier DOWN only changed HUD. Let me check if that was because W-box was at different position.
# Previously W-box was at rows 30-32 when I tried DOWN. Now it's at rows 27-29.
# Let me try DOWN again

action(['DOWN'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
